pattern: Both conditions are simple membership and position checks on the digits of the number, so peeling the number into its digit sequence once answers both at the same time.
hints: Both rules are about individual digits of n, so it helps to look at n one digit at a time rather than as a whole number.
Break n down into its list of digits (most significant first); the second rule is just about the first entry of that list, and the first rule is about whether x shows up anywhere in the list at all.
The answer is true exactly when x appears somewhere in the digit list AND the first digit of that list is not x.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, digit present and not leading
statement example, digit is the leading digit
statement example, digit not present at all
n is zero, x is zero: the single digit is both present and leading
n is zero, x is not present
n at the maximum constraint with x as its leading digit
n at the maximum constraint with x present but not leading
single-digit n equal to x: present but also leading, so the answer is false
x appears only in the interior of n, never at the front

stress.description: random n up to the constraint maximum paired with every possible digit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, max(0, min(size, 100000)))
    x = rng.randint(0, 9)
    return [n, x]
